description: A bounded undertaking organized around a stated purpose, gathering tasks, participants, materials, and stages under one name. Its boundary can move while the work lives. A project is a shape for effort, not a promise: authorization, funding, and the acceptance of the people affected are separate sentences.
sound_symbolism: Light 'ri' flicks the tongue into bright 'i', while stressed 'no' gathers the middle into a rounded nasal enclosure. In final 'ka', the closure moves back to the velum and releases through broad 'a'. The word crosses three clear mouth positions and lands on an open, practical stroke.
grammatical_notes: A countable noun: 'lo mia ha rinoka winora.' (We plan this project.) State its purpose, boundary, participants, source, tasks, dependencies, and expected result where relevant. Distinct from 'riporu' (task), 'terura' (system), 'punoki' (institution), and 'seru' (commitment).

pillars.solarpunk-values: Community projects can coordinate repair, care, construction, and ecological work while remaining open to revision and criticism.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Separating the undertaking from its authority and participation relations keeps a shared purpose from being treated as blanket consent.

tags.activity: a bounded undertaking containing related work
tags.community: a possible frame for coordinated participation
tags.creation: an undertaking directed toward a stated result
